item.text:
[demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/demo/rate_limiter.py:15) already has the minimal fix in this checkout:

```python
self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
```

The leak happens when `self.calls` only ever appends successful request timestamps. Old timestamps outside the rate-limit window no longer affect throttling, but if they stay in the list forever, every accepted request permanently grows memory. The minimal fix is to prune expired timestamps at the start of `allow()` before checking `len(self.calls)`.

Patch form:

```diff
 def allow(self) -> bool:
     now = time.time()
+    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
     if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
         self.calls.append(now)
         return True
     return False
```

I verified the existing test passes: `python3 -m unittest tests.test_rate_limiter`.

--- Learning check ---

Why should the pruning happen before `len(self.calls) < self.limit`, rather than only after a request is accepted?
